frameworks.name: event segmentation
frameworks.one_line: continuous experience is parsed into discrete events at perceived boundaries

frameworks.name: hippocampal-prefrontal memory integration-separation
frameworks.one_line: HPC-PFC interplay binds or separates sequential representations across event boundaries

frameworks.name: episodic memory reactivation
frameworks.one_line: retroactive integration via reactivation links past content into present context

core_question: How do event boundaries shape hippocampal and prefrontal mechanisms that organize continuous experience into discrete episodic memories?
key_assumptions: perceived boundaries arise from change in thoughts, goals, or surroundings
temporal stability and change together scaffold mnemonic structure
long-term episodic memory depends on hippocampal-prefrontal interaction
manipulations: novel versus familiar sequences of information
presence versus absence of contextual shifts at boundaries
key_findings: event boundaries modulate hippocampal and prefrontal mechanisms supporting episodic memory
hippocampus integrates and separates sequential information around boundaries
hippocampal processes operate both independently and jointly with other regions to bind sequences
interpretations: discrete episodic memories form proactively as experience unfolds and retroactively via context shifts or reactivation
temporal stability and change provide scaffolding for HPC-PFC processes that link and separate memories
